title: glochidion runikerae
intro: Glochidion runikerae, also known as the Rau Kapok, is a type of plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. It can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
